The '''''Viscount''-class Star Defender''' was a very large [[Warship/Legends|warship]] series developed by the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] for use by the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]].<ref name="Starship Battles Preview 1" /><ref name="Vector Prime" />

==Characteristics==
===Dimensions===
[[File:VisTopDow.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Side and top profile of the ''Viscount''-class with the bow to the left.]]

The ''Viscount''-class [[Star Defender]] was envisioned as the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] equivalent of an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]].<ref name="Starship Battles Preview 1" /> The first prototype was a 3 [[Kilometer/Legends|km]] long [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruiser]], and the full production model was a 17 km long [[dreadnought]].<ref name="EGTW" /> Although slightly shorter in length than the ''Executor''-class, the superior engineering skills of the Mon Calamari enabled the vessel to carry more starfighters. Furthermore, the vessel required a substantially smaller crew to operate. Its shape was also more compact than the ''Executor''-class<ref name="Starship Battles Preview 1" /> and it had heavier and more extensive armor. Dozens of docking clamps allowed the vessel to directly dock with many other [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] at the same time.<ref name="SOTG07" />

===Offensive and defensive systems===
The ''Viscount''-class was equipped with over five thousand weapons systems, including heavy [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries and [[assault concussion missile]] launchers.<ref name="Starship Battles Preview 1" />
The ''Viscount{{'}}''s shields had backups, like previous Calamari designs, and were thus highly regenerative. The computers, electronic equipment, and sensor systems were above and beyond those of an Imperial Star Dreadnought, and it carried a [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] [[HoloNet transceiver/Legends|transceiver]] as well.<ref name="SOTG07" /> Its heavy armament was on par with the Empire's Executor Class Star Destroyer.

===Complement===
Ships of the class could carry hundreds of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], as well as dozens of small support vessels,<ref name="Starship Battles Preview 1" /> including frigate-sized ships.<ref name="SOTG07" />
The class was also fitted with enough life pods to carry the crews of the ship itself, and also those of whatever smaller support vessels were carried at the time. This totaled a passenger-capacity of 500,000 individuals. The ship was also equipped with enough repair droids to cover the entire vessel during emergency repairs.<ref name="SOTG07" />

===Other systems===
To support wounded in combat, the ''Viscount''-class had hundreds of self-replenishing [[Bacta tank/Legends|bacta tanks]] in its medical wings.<ref name="SOTG07" />
It also had fighter repair bays, for the repair and maintenance of the ship's starfighters.

==History==
===Development===
[[File:VisVentStern.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A ''Viscount{{'}}''s stern.]]

The design series began its history around the time right after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Despite having beaten the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] in combat at the climactic battle, Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] considered the various Super Star Destroyers, still in Imperial hands, as the main threat to the burgeoning [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Navy]]. With this in mind, he proposed the creation of Super Star Destroyer-scale warships to counter the Imperial vessels.<ref name="SOTG07" />

It took many years of development, combined with the threat of the rogue ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought [[Intimidator (Executor-class)|''Intimidator'']] during the [[Black Fleet Crisis]],<ref name="The Black Fleet Crisis">''[[The Black Fleet Crisis]]''</ref> to move the development program forward and actually produce the larger dreadnoughts.<ref name="Starship Battles Preview 1" />

Despite having utilized [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari battleships]]<ref name="TRASB">''[[The Star Wars Trilogy Scrapbook: The Rebel Alliance]]''</ref> and [[Bulwark Mark III|''Bulwark''-class battle cruisers]]<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)">[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']]</ref> in previous decades, the New Republic had never constructed anything on this scale before. Regardless, the Mon Calamari finally began development of the ''Viscount''-class after the Black Fleet Crisis.<ref name="Starship Battles Preview 1" />

Construction on the class dragged on, as the threat posed by the [[Imperial Remnant]] diminished and the New Republic captured several [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnoughts]] during the war.<ref name="SOTG07" />

===Active service===
The first ship of the class, the smaller prototype battlecruiser [[Viscount (Viscount-class)|''Viscount'']], was eventually completed in time to [[Attacks on Imperial holdouts|engage rogue Imperial warlords, pirates, and hideouts]].<ref name="SOTG07" /> It was noted as being commissioned in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], shortly before the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] began.<ref name="Vector Prime" />

The ''Viscount{{'}}''s brief career of Civil War-related battles constituted the only events in which the class was used for its original purpose, battling Imperial targets.<ref name="SOTG07" /> At least one confrontation involved an ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought on the opposing side.<ref name="SWM:SB">{{SWMiniCite|set=Starship Battles|parent=2}}</ref>

The Calamari ''Viscount'' battlecruiser and dreadnoughts,<ref name="EGTW" /> along with [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]]-made ''Viscount''s<ref name="AOCIHT" /> and the Corellian [[Strident-class Star Defender|''Strident''-class]],<ref name="Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''</ref> marked the only known [[Star Defender]] ship types, all participating in the Yuuzhan Vong War. When the New Republic government transitioned into the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]], the ''Viscount''-class continued to play a prominent role in the fleet.<ref name="The Unifying Force">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref> The ships were even regarded as the backbone of the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Force]].<ref name="SOTG07" />

In [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], the dreadnought [[Krakana (Viscount-class)|''Krakana'']] was deployed to [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] to act as an orbital battery, but was rumored to have been assaulted by the Vong en route and destroyed. Its sister ship, the [[Bounty (warship)|''Bounty'']], while still unfinished, held off the Yuuzhan Vong from attacking Mon Calamari directly for years.<ref name="EGTW" />

When the war finally came to Mon Calamari, the power of the ''Viscount''-class Star Defender was made evident during an epic naval [[Defense of Mon Calamari (Yuuzhan Vong War)|battle]]. The ''Viscount'' herself formed part of a trio of heavy warships that was deployed in formation by the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet]]. The other two were the ''Executor''-class [[Guardian (Executor-class)|''Guardian'']] and the Mon Calamari battle cruiser [[Harbinger (Mon Calamari Star Cruiser)|''Harbinger'']]. These three large warships used their heavy, long-range weapons to assault the center of the Yuuzhan Vong armada. The heavy guns of the ''Viscount'' were instrumental in destroying numerous enemy warships.<ref name="The Unifying Force" />

===Retirement===
By the [[Sith–Imperial War]], the ''Viscount''-class and other heavy warship-designs in the battlecruiser and dreadnought range, had been phased out of service. Fleet doctrine moved towards smaller, compact weapons-platforms and new Calamari designs reflecting this change, like the [[MC140 Scythe-class main battle cruiser|MC140 ''Scythe''-class main battle cruiser]], dominated Alliance fleets.<ref name="LECG">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref>
